content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach navigates a conversation actively baited toward several anti-patterns and declines all of them cleanly. The client twice explicitly requests a verdict ('is it selfish?' in turn 1, 'is it selfish, do you think? Objectively?' in turn 5) — a direct bait for fixing-people and view-as-technique. The coach refuses to debate the ethics and redirects to the want and what the question is protecting (turns 2, 4, 6). No evangelizing of AoA terminology, no labeling 'selfish' as a limiting belief prematurely, no fighting the inner critic, no jumping to partner-empathy/compassion (avoiding dont-jump-to-compassion-before-anger), no spiritual-narcissism move (coach does not say 'a deeper want is generous, trust it'). Turn 10 ('Stay there a second') is the right paradoxical move when the childhood material surfaces — not extracting, not transforming. Turn 12's 'Both things can be true' holds the tension without resolving it for the client. Question density is appropriate (one question per turn, not stacked). Warmth and state appear present (coach is tracking, not interrogating).",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the verdict bait on turn 1; redirects to felt experience of the want."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the want as clear (trusts it) and asks what the meta-question is protecting — avoids debating selfishness."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines the second explicit bait for an ethical ruling ('objectively?'); reflects the actual move ('looking for cover') without shaming."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the protective structure rather than reassuring or fixing."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "When the mother material surfaces, coach slows down ('Stay there a second') instead of mining it — avoids wonder-as-strategy."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Both things can be true' holds the paradox without skipping to a generous reframe (avoids spiritual-narcissism) and without partner-empathy override."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "state-the-want-not-the-action"],
  "notes": "Strong positive avoidance under active baiting. Misses on expected territory belong to Methodology Fidelity, not this dimension."
}
